---
title: "Introduction"
---

# Project Description

Understanding how personal values shape political attitudes is a key question in political sociology. In Europe, this relationship has been tested by major societal changes, particularly the COVID-19 pandemic. This project examines whether such disruptions have altered the link between human values and political orientations across generations.

# Data Sources

The analysis uses data from the European Social Survey (Rounds 9–11). We construct value indices based on the Schwartz framework and analyze their relationship with political attitudes using descriptive statistics and statistical tests.

# Sections

This project is structured into several sections, each addressing a specific part of the analysis and research questions.


- The [Literature Review](literature_review.ipynb) provides the theoretical background based on Schwartz’s Theory of Basic Human Values and discusses previous empirical findings.
- The empirical analysis is presented in dedicated sections (the research questions), where we explore the relationship between human values and political attitudes using European Social Survey data.
- The [Conclusion](conclusion.qmd) summarizes the key findings and reflects on their implications for understanding political behavior in Europe.

The Research Questions are:

1. [To what extent do Schwartz’s 10 basic human values predict political attitudes across European countries, and which values have the greatest impact?](sibahle_question_1.ipynb)

2. [How do political attitudes and value priorities differ across generational cohorts (Gen Z, Millennials, Gen X, and Boomers) in the Pre and post pandemic landscape?](Q2_Tayaphon.ipynb)

3. [Did the COVID-19 pandemic lead to a measurable shift in 'Conservation' values (Security, Conformity, Tradition) and 'Political Trust' between Round 9 and Round 11?](pelin_analysis.ipynb)

4. [Does the link between personal values and political orientation become stronger or weaker as respondents age, or is the relationship fixed within generational cohorts?](Felix-Analysis.ipynb)

5. [How does the value of 'Universalism' predict attitudes toward government-led income redistribution, and has this relationship changed for Gen Z compared to previous generations?](Uyazi_Q5.ipynb)

6. [To what extent does the correlation between human values and political attitudes vary between different European countries?](Maria_Question_6.ipynb)
